title: GitHub Profile, Repository Health, and Verifiable Work · Career Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed hiring review reconstructs github profile, repository health, and verifiable work from target, claim, evidence, audience, action, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, consent, owner, and next-decision evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for selective GitHub evidence route after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure.
Write a concise profile route that helps a reviewer reach role, proof, boundaries, contact choice, and accessible alternatives.
Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reproducible Proof
steps.instruction:
review case — A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The review variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.

Inspect the Reproducible Proof trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reproducible Proof.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reproducible Proof, keep Reproducible Proof connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Reproducible Proof, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reproducible Proof, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Reproducible Proof precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reproducible Proof, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The review variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reproducible Proof, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A passing historical workflow badge proves the project works now.

stimulus.title: Reproducible Proof case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The review variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A passing historical workflow badge proves the project works now.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route. Artifact under review: selective GitHub evidence route.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Reproducible Proof, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Reproducible Proof.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Project Selection

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Reproducible Proof
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Reproducible Proof: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The review variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Reproducible Proof.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Reproducible Proof, keep Reproducible Proof connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.
